mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the equity risk premium and discount rates applied to future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cutting periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering risk-off repricing that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong residential construction activity amplifies the positive impact of robust industrial production on aggregate demand, creating a synergistic demand shock that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising housing starts and industrial production fails to produce a positive rank correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns exceeding 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, a rebound in industrial production directly translates to higher capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with INDPRO rebounds during identified post-shock recovery periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates fail to exhibit a positive rank correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns exceeding 0.04 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches elevated inflation thresholds, the market anticipates forced monetary tightening and compressed real yields, which suppresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 208 threshold, contradicting the inflation-suppression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong non-farm payroll growth and stable unemployment rates confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which validates sustained consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE fails to produce a positive predictive rank IC > 0.03 for 20-day forward SPX returns over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Surging residential mortgage debt outstanding indicates active credit expansion and rising household leverage, which fuels near-term consumption demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week during credit expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive relationship with RSAFS levels above 380 trillion during identified credit expansion regimes (p > 0.05).
